title: Add a student
summary: Add a student in Inserting Data

sketchBlocks.title: Inserting a New Row
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
To add a new row to a table in SQL, use the `INSERT INTO` statement. It's best practice to specify the columns you want to insert values into, especially when the table has many columns or defaults. For example, to add a new inventory item:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES ('Student Badge', 'Accessories', 2.50, 'active', 0, 'Welcome gift', '2026-02-10');
```

This statement adds a new row to the `inventory_items` table with the provided values for each column.

sketchBlocks.title: Why Specify Columns?
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: Specifying columns in your `INSERT` statement makes your code safer and more readable. If the table structure changes (for example, if a new column is added), your statement will still work as long as you provide values for the columns you list. Omitting the column list can lead to errors if the order of columns changes or if there are columns with default values.

quizDraft.title: Insert a new student badge
quizDraft.prompt:
Write an SQL statement to add a new row to the `inventory_items` table with these values:
- name: 'Student Badge'
- category: 'Accessories'
- price: 2.50
- status: 'active'
- is_test: 0
- notes: 'Welcome gift'
- last_updated: '2026-02-10'

Use an explicit column list. Do not provide a value for `id`.
quizDraft.hint: Use INSERT INTO with a column list and VALUES.

help.concept: The INSERT INTO statement adds a new row to a table. It's safer to specify the columns you are inserting into.
help.hint_1: List all columns except id in parentheses after the table name.
help.hint_2: Match the order of columns in your list to the order of values you provide.

quizDraft.starterCode: -- Write your SQL INSERT statement here
quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES ('Student Badge', 'Accessories', 2.50, 'active', 0, 'Welcome gift', '2026-02-10');

quizDraft.title: Insert a test item with minimal info
quizDraft.prompt:
Insert a new row into `inventory_items` with the following values:
- name: 'Test Keychain'
- category: 'Accessories'
- price: 1.00
- status: 'draft'
- is_test: 1
- notes: NULL
- last_updated: '2026-02-11'

Do not insert a value for `id`. Use NULL for the notes column.
quizDraft.hint: Remember to use NULL for the notes column and specify all columns except id.

help.concept: You can insert NULL into a column by writing NULL as the value in your VALUES list.
help.hint_1: Include notes in your column list and use NULL in its place in VALUES.
help.hint_2: Make sure the order of columns matches the order of values.

quizDraft.starterCode: -- Insert a test item with NULL notes
quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES ('Test Keychain', 'Accessories', 1.00, 'draft', 1, NULL, '2026-02-11');

quizDraft.title: Insert an item using default values
quizDraft.prompt: Add a new row to `inventory_items` for a 'Student Notebook' in the 'Accessories' category, priced at 3.00. Let the `status`, `is_test`, and `last_updated` columns use their default values. Set `notes` to NULL. Do not provide a value for `id`.
quizDraft.hint: You can omit columns that have defaults if you want to use those defaults.

help.concept: If a column has a default value, you can leave it out of the column list and SQL will use the default.
help.hint_1: List only the columns you want to provide values for.
help.hint_2: Omit status, is_test, and last_updated from your column list to use their defaults.

quizDraft.starterCode: -- Insert a notebook, using defaults for some columns
quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, notes)
VALUES ('Student Notebook', 'Accessories', 3.00, NULL);

quizDraft.title: Choosing columns for INSERT
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following are good reasons to always specify a column list in your INSERT statements? Select all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Think about table changes and code clarity.

help.concept: Specifying columns in INSERT statements makes your code safer and more maintainable.
help.hint_1: Consider what happens if the table structure changes.
help.hint_2: Think about how specifying columns can prevent errors and improve readability.

options.a: It prevents errors if the table structure changes.
options.b: It makes the code easier to read and maintain.
options.c: It automatically fills in missing values with zeros.
options.d: It ensures values go into the correct columns.

quizDraft.title: Default values in INSERT
quizDraft.prompt: When you omit a column from the column list in an INSERT statement, SQL will use the column's ____ if it has one.
quizDraft.hint: Think about what SQL uses when you don't provide a value.

help.concept: If a column has a default value and you don't specify it in your INSERT, SQL uses the default.
help.hint_1: SQL will not leave the value empty if a default is set.
help.hint_2: The database uses the value defined in the table schema for that column.

quizDraft.template: When you omit a column from the column list in an INSERT statement, SQL will use the column's ____ if it has one.
